title: Game controller extraction wave
summary: Reduce the biggest coordinator risk without changing player-facing scope.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: One meaningful responsibility leaves game.ts, with behavior covered and no new runtime framework added.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Extract one cohesive controller/helper from game.ts around notices, expedition activation, route filing, or overlay action dispatch.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Avoid systems files; provide only copy/test fixtures needed by the extraction.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Avoid controller files; run spatial smoke proof for any changed action dispatch touching traversal/inspect.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Add route-loop regression tests around the extracted seam and confirm support/filing priority is unchanged.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

finding: The guided field-season notice policy in game.ts is pure enough to extract without changing runtime flow.
field-notices.ts already owns adjacent notice policy helpers, so the extraction has a natural home.
Existing focused runtime smoke covers the first field-season guidance and season-capstone notice flow that this policy gates.
main_scope: Move guided notice title classification into src/engine/field-notices.ts.
Export a pure canShowGuidedFieldSeasonNotice(currentNotice, nextTitle) helper that mirrors current game.ts behavior.
Replace game.ts local helper calls with the exported helper and delete the local helper functions.

Add src/test/field-notices.test.ts coverage for guided-title stacking, empty-current-notice behavior, non-guided current notice blocking, filed-route current notice blocking, and non-guided next titles.
non_goals: No notice text, notice duration, field-request completion, route filing, expedition activation, station behavior, support behavior, save schema, overlay render behavior, authored science/content, biome geometry, class, new controller framework, subscription, or global mutable notice state changes.
baseline_checks: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "field-season guidance|season capstone"

result: Moved guided field-season notice title classification into src/engine/field-notices.ts.
Added a pure canShowGuidedFieldSeasonNotice(currentNotice, nextTitle) helper and wired game.ts to use it.
Removed the local guided-notice helper functions from game.ts.
Added unit coverage for guided title recognition and guided notice stacking/replacement rules.
scope_notes: No notice copy, notice duration, field-request completion, route filing, expedition activation, station behavior, support behavior, save schema, overlay rendering, authored science/content, biome geometry, class, new framework, subscription, or global mutable notice controller changed.

findings: The extracted helper mirrors the old guided notice behavior.
game.ts now delegates guided notice title policy to field-notices.ts at every previous call site.
Focused unit and runtime-smoke coverage protect the extracted seam.

finding: Packet 150 lane 1 extracted guided field-season notice title policy into field-notices.ts.
Packet 150 lane 4 added route-critical notice-priority coverage in field-notices.test.ts.

Neither completed pass intentionally touched movement, climb, travel-target, or inspect selection dispatch, so lane 3 should stay no-code and leave a focused spatial audit/proof report instead of editing controller files.
main_scope: Do not edit src/engine/game.ts, src/engine/field-notices.ts, traversal helpers, biome geometry, renderer code, route state, station surfaces, save schema, or authored content.
Add a short implementation/report note confirming no traversal or inspect action-dispatch change landed in packet 150.
Rerun the focused existing runtime smoke slice: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play, inspect".
Update packet/queue/progress metadata and promote ECO-20260420-critic-408 if the proof is clean.
non_goals: No controller extraction, helper movement, test rewrite, browser screenshot, generated output, route/support behavior, notice policy change, geometry change, inspect-target algorithm change, climb/travel behavior change, station/menu change, save migration, or content-copy edit.
baseline_checks: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play, inspect"
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play, inspect"

result: Confirmed packet 150 did not require traversal or inspect action-dispatch edits for lane 3.

Left controller files, traversal helpers, biome geometry, renderer code, route/support behavior, notice policy, inspect-target algorithms, climb/travel behavior, station/menu behavior, save schema, tests, generated output, and authored content unchanged.
Reran the focused existing runtime smoke slice covering title/play/inspect behavior.
verification: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play, inspect"

findings: The lane-3 packet-150 response stayed appropriately no-code because the completed extraction/test work did not touch traversal or inspect action dispatch.
The existing title/play/inspect runtime-smoke slice still passes after packet-150 notice policy work.

No controller files, traversal helpers, biome geometry, renderer code, route/support behavior, notice policy, inspect-target algorithms, climb/travel behavior, station/menu behavior, save schema, tests, generated output, or authored content changed in this lane-3 main step.
verification: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "covers title, play, inspect"

finding: Lane 1 already extracted guided field-season notice policy into src/engine/field-notices.ts.
Focused baseline coverage for field-notices plus field-season guidance/season-capstone runtime smoke passes.
Existing tests cover guided notice blocking and station support priority separately, but no single route-critical matrix ties both extracted helper seams together.
main_scope: Add one focused test in src/test/field-notices.test.ts that treats notebook-ready and filed-route as route-critical notices across canShowGuidedFieldSeasonNotice(...) and shouldReplaceFieldNotice(...).
Cover guided notice blocking for at least one guided next title.
Cover station default/support blocking and filed-over-ready replacement.
Keep the pass test-only unless the new test exposes a real helper bug.
non_goals: No game.ts, controller extraction, runtime behavior, notice copy, notice durations, route definitions, support behavior, save schema, station layout/state, overlay rendering, authored science/content, biome geometry, new framework, or broad runtime-smoke scenario changes.
baseline_checks: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/field-notices.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "field-season guidance|season capstone|station support|guided notices|filed-route"

result: Added a focused route-critical notice-priority regression across canShowGuidedFieldSeasonNotice(...) and shouldReplaceFieldNotice(...).
Confirmed guided notices cannot replace notebook-ready or filed-route notices.
Confirmed station support/default toasts cannot replace notebook-ready or filed-route while the field-station overlay is open.
Confirmed filed-route can still replace notebook-ready when the route is filed.
scope_notes: No game.ts, controller extraction, runtime behavior, notice copy, notice duration, route definition, support behavior, save schema, station layout/state, overlay rendering, authored science/content, biome geometry, new framework, or broad runtime-smoke changes.
verification: Passed: npm run validate:agents with the known work-queue-size warning

findings: The new route-critical regression ties notebook-ready and filed-route notices to both canShowGuidedFieldSeasonNotice(...) and shouldReplaceFieldNotice(...).
Guided FIELD STATION / SEASON THREADS notices cannot replace route-critical notices.
Station support/default notices cannot replace notebook-ready or filed-route while the field-station overlay is open.
Filed-route can still replace notebook-ready when the route is filed.
The implementation stayed test-only for runtime behavior with no route, support, save, station, overlay, content, geometry, framework, or broad runtime-smoke drift.
verification: Passed: npm run validate:agents with the known work-queue-size warning
